![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Budget: Real Cost and 2026 Tips](https://photos.parkoria.app/magic-tips/cruise/disney-adventure-small.webp)

# Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Budget: Real Cost and 2026 Tips

5 min Guide 2026 Hong Kong Disneyland Chine · Lantau Updated May 22, 2026

Planning a trip to **Hong Kong Disneyland** in 2026 is a great idea. The resort is compact, the Hong Kong dollar (HKD) offers a stable exchange rate, and the cost of living is generally lower than the standards of Disneyland Paris or Walt Disney World. However, the cost of park tickets, the strategic choice of hotels (on site or in town) and the management of paid extras such as skip-the-line (*Premier Access*) or early access (*Early Park Entry*) can heavily impact your final budget. This guide gives you all the real numbers and our tips for saving.

The key tip in 15 seconds

## Choose the official 2-Day Fun ticket and keep an eye out for hotel + ticket package offers on the official HKDL or Klook website.

The price difference between a 1-day ticket and a 2-day ticket is minimal (around 100 HKD difference). Additionally, booking a package combining the Disney hotel and tickets allows you to obtain major discounts of up to 30% on the overall stay.

Estimated Budget—HKDL 2026

* ✦ 1-day park ticket: HKD 639 to HKD 879 (approximately €75 to €105) depending on the seasonal rate
* ✦ 2-day offer (Non-stop 2-Day Fun Ticket): The best option at around €125 (excellent ratio)
* ✦ Disney Hotel on site: From €130/night at the Hollywood Hotel, up to €350+/night at the Disneyland Hotel
* ✦ Catering in the park: HKD 120 to HKD 250 per meal (approximately €14 to €30 per person)
* ✦ Economical alternative: Stay in Kowloon or Tung Chung (from €60 to €90 per night)
* ✦ Estimated total budget: $950–$2,000 for 2 people (5 days)

[Crowd snapshot → Check the crowd calendar](/en/crowd-calendar/hong-kong-disneyland-resort)

Our expert opinion

**Updated 2026.** The Hong Kong resort is halfway between the very moderate cost of Tokyo and the exorbitant rates of Florida or Paris. Thanks to very aggressive hotel deals (up to 30% off when booking in advance) and the possibility of off-site accommodation without losing accessibility, HKDL is one of the easiest Disney parks to budget intelligently.

Recommended guides

* →← [The unmissable attractions (must-do)](must-do-rides-hkdl-attractions-incontournables)
* →← [Logistics & Transport Guide](logistics-hk-visa-transport-octopus)

## 1. Entrance tickets: 2026 prices and optimal purchasing channels

Hong Kong Disneyland operates a four-tier seasonal pricing system (Tier 1 to Tier 4) for its 1-day tickets. Prices range from HKD 639 (around €75) in low season to HKD 879 (around €105) for the busiest days of the year.

**The Non-stop 2-Day Fun Ticket:** This is the star product. Available on the official website and at authorized resellers until September 30, 2026, it costs around 1,068 HKD (€125) and entitles you to 2 full days of access. The difference with the 1 day high season ticket is minimal.

**Klook and KKday:** These approved Asian platforms regularly offer promotions and flash sales on tickets and meal vouchers (Meal Vouchers), often 5% to 10% cheaper than official box office prices.

✨

Reservation required

Even with a ticket purchased on Klook, you must register your visit date on the official Hong Kong Disneyland website or risk being refused entry.

## 2. Accommodation: Disney Hotels vs. City Accommodations

Sleeping on site in Hong Kong is a surprising option: it is one of the rare places in the world where official Disney hotels offer very competitive value for money, especially outside weekends.

**Disney's Hollywood Hotel:** Completely renovated with colorful Art Deco style and Marvel touches, this is the most economical option at the resort. Rooms often start at €130 to €160 per night.

**Disney Explorers Lodge:** A traveler's favorite. Inspired by expeditions to the Amazon and Africa, it offers a complete change of scenery with its large tropical gardens and spectacular outdoor swimming pool, for around €180 to €220 per night.

✨

Promotional package

The official website has the 'Advance Purchase Room Offer' offering up to 30% off the room if you book at least 21 days in advance.

## 3. Catering & Souvenirs: Manage daily costs

Eating at Hong Kong Disneyland is more affordable than in the United States or Paris, but prices are still those of a theme park. Expect around 120 to 180 HKD (€14 to €21) for a main course in a [quick service](/en/glossaire/quick-service) restaurant.

**Meal Vouchers:** Available for purchase on Klook, these coupons cost around €25 and include a main meal with a drink in the parks, plus a snack (popcorn or ice cream). This is a great way to save around 15% on food.

**At-table restaurants:** If you are aiming for emblematic tables like the gourmet restaurant *Crystal Lotus* (for its famous Dim Sum in the shape of Disney characters) or the characters [buffet](/en/glossaire/buffet-disney) *Chef Mickey*, count on a more substantial budget of around €45 to €70 per adult.

✨

Save on snacks

The park allows the bringing of small snacks (cookies, cereal bars, compotes) for children. Take advantage of this to avoid compulsive purchases during the day.

## 4. Paid Options: Useful investments or unnecessary expenses?

Unlike American or Parisian parks where paid skip-the-line tickets are almost essential to do everything, Hong Kong Disneyland offers a much more relaxed experience.

**Disney Premier Access (DPA):** This individual or pack skip-the-line pass (3 or 8 attractions) costs from 229 HKD (27 €) to 549 HKD (65 €). It is only recommended if you visit the park on a single day during Chinese school holidays or weekends.

**Early Park Entry Pass:** For 199 HKD (around €23), this pass allows you to enter the park 1 hour before the official opening. This is the most profitable investment of the stay: it allows you to do Frozen Ever After and Sliding Sleighs without any waiting, saving more than 2 hours in line during the day.

✨

Expert arbitration

Buy Early Entry as a priority. The €23 invested will save you the purchase of a much more expensive Premier Access pass later in the day.

## 5. Frequently asked questions

What is the average budget for 2 days at Hong Kong Disneyland? 

Allow around **€300 to 450 per person** for 2 days, including the 2-day ticket (around €125), a night at the Hollywood Hotel or the Explorers Lodge (€130 to 220), meals and miscellaneous costs. By staying in Kowloon, you can get down to €200 to €300 per person all inclusive.

Is it better to stay in a Disney hotel or in town in Kowloon? 

If you come only for the park, Disney hotels offer excellent value for money (notably the Explorers Lodge from €180). If you want to visit Hong Kong at the same time, Kowloon makes more sense: hotels there cost €60 to €90/night and the MTR takes you to the park in 40 minutes for around €3.

Can you pay by credit card in HKDL? 

**Yes**, Visa and Mastercard are accepted everywhere in the park. The **Octopus Card** (reloadable Hong Kong transport card) is also accepted and practical for small purchases. It is recommended to load one at the airport.

Is World of Frozen worth the detour? 

**Absolutely**. Opening in 2023, World of Frozen is HKDL's most impressive themed area. The signature attraction *Frozen Ever After* (boat) and the *Sliding Sleighs* are unique to Hong Kong. Prioritize this area upon opening or with Early Entry.

Do I have to buy tickets in advance? 

**Yes, highly recommended.** Purchasing in advance (via the official website or Klook) guarantees your entry date, which is required to access the park. In high season, some dates close several weeks in advance. In addition, platforms like Klook offer discounts of 5 to 10% compared to counters.

My personal favorite item in Golden Crocus Inn, the Forest Mushroom Strozzapetti Pasta is a creamy, flavorful delight. This is the one vegetarian option on the menu, and if you’re craving some pasta, this is probably the best pasta in the park. The sauce is just so creamy and bursting with flavor. This is my go-to order whenever I dine at Golden Crocus Inn. I definitely recommend it if you decide to dine there, especially because it is also the cheapest dish in Golden Crocus Inn. 

## Olaf Ice Cream from Northern Delights, World of Frozen

It also comes with a bunch of different sides that you can try. Wayfinder’s Table in general is a great place to eat and its menu doesn’t have any duds. ## The Best Food at Hong Kong Disneyland 2026

![Best Food at Hong Kong Disneyland cover](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/HKDL-Trip-Report-2023-Part-One-Introduction-91.png "HKDL Trip Report 2023 Part One Introduction (91)")

Hong Kong Disneyland (HKDL) probably has the most diverse set of food options of any Disney theme park. While your standard theme park fare like fried chicken and burgers are present here, Hong Kong Disneyland also has dishes that take inspiration from nearby cultures and cuisines. Things like Pho and Ramen are available at Hong Kong Disneyland. Because the food is so diverse, I thought I would make a post ranking the best food at Hong Kong Disneyland. 

From quick bites to savory delicacies, there’s no shortage of good eats at Hong Kong Disneyland. I will preface this by saying that while I have not tried every single food item in Hong Kong Disneyland, I have probably had every single notable food item. Of course there are hundreds of them scattered around the resort, but I think I was able to come up with a list that perfectly represents the diversity and quality of the food in the park. 

Please note that these dishes and snacks are in no particular order and they all represent the quality of food at Hong Kong Disneyland. In terms of overall food, I think HKDL has the best food among the Asia parks, but is tied or slightly lower than the American parks. But still, HKDL has some high quality and unique food items that you need to try when you visit. 

If you’d like to purchase some discounted Hong Kong Disneyland dining offers, [click here](https://klook.tpm.li/9lcIRTFk).

You can also read my [Hong Kong Disneyland Snack Guide](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/top-10-hong-kong-disneyland-snacks-you-need-to-try-hkdl-snack-ranking/) and my [Hong Kong Disneyland Quick Service Ranking](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/best-hong-kong-disneyland-counter-service-restaurants/).

## **Caramel Popcorn from Various Popcorn Carts**

Current Price: HK$48 / US$6.18

You can’t make a Best Food at Hong Kong Disneyland list and not include one of the most iconic snacks found in Hong Kong Disneyland. Caramel Popcorn has been a staple of the park since it opened in 2005. It’s warm, sweet, and very caramel-y. It’s the perfect snack to take with you as you walk around the park. You can even buy collectible popcorn buckets with it. Sometimes they even have unique flavors like Dark Chocolate or Caramel-Banana. You can never go wrong with popcorn, which is why it’s on this list. 

## **Popcorn Chicken Nachos from Lucky Nugget Saloon, Grizzly Gulch**

Current Price: HK$95 / US$12.23

With crispy nachos, tasty chicken, delectable cheese, and a kick of spice, the Popcorn Chicken Nachos from Lucky Nugget Saloon in Grizzly Gulch is a savory delight. The way the flavors all interact with one another makes it a must-eat snack at Hong Kong Disneyland. Just be aware that the peppers do make it kind of spicy so if that’s not your thing you may want to skip this. If it is your thing though then make sure you try this when you’re at the park. 

[Klook.com](//www.klook.com/)

## **Churro Bites from Lucky Nugget Saloon, Grizzly Gulch**

Current Price: HK$80 / US$10.30

Another incredible snack from Lucky Nugget Saloon, the Churro Bites are sweet-heaven. Coming with a chocolate dipping sauce, the mix of cinnamon and chocolate makes it incredibly delicious. It’s the perfect sweet treat to have after a meal at a nearby restaurant. It may be a bit on the pricier side for churros, but considering you also get a chocolate dipping sauce with it I think it’s incredibly good value.

## **Innkeeper’s Special Braised Lamb Shank from Golden Crocus Inn, World of Frozen**

Current Price: HK$248 / US$31.68

Probably the most expensive meal at any quick service restaurant at Hong Kong Disneyland, the Innkeeper’s Special Braised Lamb Shank is worth the price. Lathered in beer gravy, the Lamb Shank also comes with potato puree and roasted vegetables. Like I said, this is the most expensive quick service item in the park, so if it’s not in your budget to order it then feel free to choose any of the other options in this restaurant. 

[Klook.com](//www.klook.com/)

## **Forest Mushroom Strozzapetti Pasta from Golden Crocus Inn, World of Frozen**

Current Price: HK$198 / US$25.27

My personal favorite item in Golden Crocus Inn, the Forest Mushroom Strozzapetti Pasta is a creamy, flavorful delight. This is the one vegetarian option on the menu, and if you’re craving some pasta, this is probably the best pasta in the park. The sauce is just so creamy and bursting with flavor. This is my go-to order whenever I dine at Golden Crocus Inn. I definitely recommend it if you decide to dine there, especially because it is also the cheapest dish in Golden Crocus Inn. 

## **Olaf Ice Cream from Northern Delights, World of Frozen**

Current Price: HK$70 / US$9.01

If there was an award for best food presentation, it would go to this cold, refreshing snack. It’s vanilla ice cream at its core, but is made a thousand times cuter because of the fact that it looks exactly like Olaf. Complete with chocolate hands and a marshmallow head, this treat is one of HKDL’s best. Just be sure to eat it quickly as it tends to melt fast. 

[Klook.com](//www.klook.com/)

## **Expo Combo from Starliner Diner, Tomorrowland**

Current Price: HK$155 / US$19.78

The first of two burgers on this list, this dish would also be in the running for best food presentation in Hong Kong Disneyland. The Expo Combo is a wagyu beef burger with potato wedges, but the buns of the burger are shaped like Iron Man’s helmet. It’s also dyed red for added effect. When I first had this in 2023, I thought the buns were really dry. But, on my most recent visit, I had it again and this time it was spectacular as the bun was more moist. If you need a good burger, would definitely recommend this one. 

## **Lotso Waffle Sandwich from Jessie’s Snack Roundup, Toy Story Land**

Current Price: HK$70 / US$9.01

This is my second favorite snack at Hong Kong Disneyland. I discovered it by accident when my sister and I wanted to get a bite to eat but all the restaurants were full. The sandwich has beef, cheese, and strawberry jam. Normally those things sound like they won’t go together, but wow was this not the perfect blend of sweet and savory. It was so good I had to go back and get another one. I highly recommend it if you’re looking for a snack in Toy Story Land. 

[Klook.com](//www.klook.com/)

## **Seafood Laksa Noodle Soup from Wayfinder’s Table, Adventureland**

Current Price: HK$170 / US$21.70

This seafood Laksa noodle soup has shrimps, scallops, squids, rice noodles, crispy noodles, and a Mickey shaped egg. The result is a pretty tasty Laksa dish. If you’re a fan of Laksa, you are going to love this. The kick isn’t as strong as other Laksas but it’s still pretty good. All dishes in this restaurant, including this dish, are Halal certified. So if that’s important to you, then make this one of the places where you will dine at the park. 

## **Pork Belly Ramen from Royal Banquet Hall, Fantasyland**

Current Price: HK$160 / US$20.43

For all you ramen fans, you’ll be happy to know that Royal Banquet Hall in Fantasyland has not one but TWO different kinds of Ramen. I focused on this one as it is the one I ordered, but the other one is just as good. Each order even comes with a side of dumplings! The ramen, especially the broth and the meat, is insanely good. Royal Banquet Hall in general has the most amount of dishes among any quick service restaurant, so I would highly recommend dining there to make the most out of the many options. 

[Klook.com](//www.klook.com/)

## **Korean Squid from Squid Cart, Adventureland**

Current Price: HK$60 / US$7.72

Probably Hong Kong Disneyland’s most iconic snack, the Korean Squid from the Adventureland food cart is simple yet memorable. It tastes very fishy, and is kind of chewy, but if you love squid or seafood you’ll definitely love this. One of the best things about buying from this stall is that you can see them preparing the squid from start to finish. This is THE quintessential HKDL snack and I recommend that you try it at least once.

## **Miso Halibut Combo from Royal Banquet Hall, Fantasyland**

Current Price: HK$155 / US$19.78

The Miso Halibut Combo is another great dish if you want to sample a wide variety of things. It comes with onigiri, crab roe salad, mushrooms, a Mickey egg, and Japanese pickles. It comes in a little bento box kind of thing, and I haven’t heard of anyone disliking this particular dish. It’s also a huge value for money because of the variety you get in the bento box. If you want to just sample a large chunk of things, definitely get this combo. 

[Klook.com](//www.klook.com/)

## **Beef Steak Mee Goreng from Wayfinder’s Table, Adventureland**

Current Price: HK$180 / US$22.97

For those of you that are a fan of Mee Goreng, this dish will hit the spot. The tenderness of the beef hits just right, and it’s my personal favorite dish from Wayfinder’s Table. It also comes with a bunch of different sides that you can try. Wayfinder’s Table in general is a great place to eat and its menu doesn’t have any duds. So if you’re craving beef while you’re in the park, this is a more than worthy place to have your fix. 

## **Fried Chicken from Starliner Diner, Tomorrowland**

Current Price: HK$145 / US$18.51

Served with french fries, the fried chicken at Starliner Diner is one of the best meals in the park. Seriously, the chicken is so juicy and crispy and flavorful that you’ll forget you’re eating theme park chicken. They used to serve it with gloves so that your fingers don’t get greasy, but they’ve since gotten rid of that. If there’s one thing you need to get when you’re at Starliner Diner, definitely make it this chicken. It also helps that this fried chicken is the cheapest thing on the menu so it’s perfect for those that want a good option at a more affordable price. 

[Klook.com](//www.klook.com/)

## **Moroccan Wagyu Beef Burger Burger from Explorer’s Club, Mystic Point**

This is the best burger in the entire park, and it’s kind of sad that you can only get it during dinner at the Explorer’s Club restaurant in Mystic Point. This wagyu beef burger comes with a variety of different toppings. It also comes with a side of waffle fries, and let me tell you this is probably my favorite meal in the entire park. The fact that it also has Albert’s face on the bun makes it even better. I highly recommend ordering this if you decide to purchase the Momentous dinner meal at this restaurant.

## **The Best Food at Hong Kong Disneyland Conclusion**

And that’s all the best food at Hong Kong Disneyland. I hope this article emboldened you to try the different unique dining offerings found in each Disney restaurant and snack stand. HKDL’s menu really is so unique compared to the American Parks. I hope you’re able to go on a culinary tasting spree during your visit. results.title: Hong Kong Disneyland Dining: A Complete Guide to Restaurants, Food & Snacks - Kowloon Vibes
results.content:
Plaza Inn at Hong Kong Disneyland

### Crystal Lotus

Located in the Hong Kong Disneyland Hotel, Crystal Lotus serves exquisite Cantonese dim sum. They offer a refined lunch set that includes dim sum, seasonal soup, stir-fried dishes, fried rice, and dessert. You’ll need to make reservations in advance and budget around HK$400 – HK$600 per person.

Crystal Lotus at the Hong Kong Disneyland Hotel

Crystal Lotus at the Hong Kong Disneyland Hotel

## Restaurants at the Hong Kong Disneyland Hotels

Besides the in-park restaurants, the resort hotels also have several dining options, including bars, buffets, and cafes, which are typically open until 10:30 PM. They are a great choice for dinner after a full day in the park. [...] ### How much should I budget for food?

I recommend budgeting at least HK$400 per person for food. This would cover about HK$150 for lunch, HK$150 for dinner, and the remaining HK$100 for popcorn, ice cream, and other themed snacks.

### Which restaurants are best for families?

During Hong Kong’s hot and humid summers, I recommend choosing restaurants with ample indoor seating. For families, Starliner Diner, Royal Banquet Hall, and Golden Crocus Inn are all excellent quick-service options with plenty of indoor space. If you have a larger budget, consider making a reservation at Plaza Inn for a comfortable dim sum experience.

### When are the peak dining hours? [...] Reservations are highly recommended, and you should budget around HK$400 – HK$600 per person for a fulfilling dinner.

Enchanted Garden Restaurant

#### Enchanted Garden Restaurant

 Hotel:Hong Kong Disneyland Hotel
 Cuisine:International Buffet
 Dining Style:Buffet
 Feature:The most popular character dining experience where you can meet and take photos with Disney characters. Reservations are essential.

Walt's Cafe

#### Walt’s Cafe

 Hotel:Hong Kong Disneyland Hotel
 Cuisine:International, Western
 Dining Style:A La Carte / Set Menu
 Feature:Inspired by the life of Walt Disney, this elegant cafe offers exquisite Western dishes and afternoon tea.

Crystal Lotus

#### Crystal Lotus

[Skip to content](#content "Skip to content")


[![Kowloon Vibes](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/kowloon-vibes-header-banner-v2.svg)](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/)

![Hong Kong Disneyland Dining: A Complete Guide to Restaurants, Food & Snacks](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-thumbnail-v1.jpg)

# Hong Kong Disneyland Dining: A Complete Guide to Restaurants, Food & Snacks

by [Kowloon Vibes Team](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/author/alan/ "View all posts by Kowloon Vibes Team")

## A Practical Guide to Restaurants and Food at Hong Kong Disneyland

How many restaurants are there in Hong Kong Disneyland? What kind of cuisine do they serve? And how much should you budget for a meal? If you’re planning a full day at the park, you’re likely wondering about the dining options and whether they’re worth the price.

In this article, I will share my experiences from multiple visits to provide an in-depth guide to dining at Hong Kong Disneyland. We’ll cover the types of restaurants, their costs, the most popular snacks, and strategies for choosing where to eat. After reading this, you’ll have a clear idea of what’s available and can plan your dining for the day in advance.

## Types of Dining at Hong Kong Disneyland

Based on official information and my own experience, there are three main types of places to get food in the park:

1. Quick-Service Restaurants and Food Courts
2. Table-Service Restaurants
3. Snack Carts and Kiosks

In short, snack kiosks offer the cheapest but simplest food options. Table-service restaurants provide the best dining experience but at a higher cost. Quick-service restaurants fall somewhere in between in terms of price and experience. Here’s a breakdown of each restaurant’s features.

## Hong Kong Disneyland Restaurant List, Cuisine & Prices

There are currently 10 main restaurants in Hong Kong Disneyland, a mix of quick-service and table-service establishments.

![Locations of the various restaurants within Hong Kong Disneyland Park](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-food-station-map-1024x576.jpg)

Locations of the various restaurants within Hong Kong Disneyland Park

Most of these restaurants offer set meals that include a main course and a drink, such as a plate of fried rice with a Coke or a bowl of noodle soup with a Coke.

Based on my experience, most set meals cost between HK$140 – HK$200, with a few exceeding HK$250. The portion sizes are generally sufficient for an average adult.

Here is a list of the restaurants, their cuisine, and typical costs:

![Plaza Inn](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-plaza-inn.jpg)

#### Plaza Inn

* **Cuisine:**Classic Cantonese dishes, Hong Kong-style dim sum, set meals, and Cantonese cuisine. The choice for an authentic Chinese meal in the park.
* **Budget:**HK$250 – $400 /person
* **Avg. Wait Time:**Reservations recommended; wait times can be 30-45 minutes during peak hours.

![Main Street Corner Cafe](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-main-street-corner-cafe.jpg)

#### Main Street Corner Cafe

* **Cuisine:**Western fare such as burgers, pasta, and steak.
* **Budget:**HK$250 – $400 /person
* **Avg. Wait Time:**Reservations recommended; wait times can be 20-30 minutes during peak hours.

![Clopin’s Festival of Foods](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-clopins-festival-of-foods.jpg)

#### Clopin’s Festival of Foods

* **Cuisine:**Common Hong Kong cafe food, such as fried rice and noodle soup.
* **Budget:**HK$140 – $200 /person
* **Avg. Wait Time:**10-20 minutes (during peak hours).

![Starliner Diner](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-starliner-diner.jpg)

#### Starliner Diner

* **Cuisine:**American classics like burgers, fried chicken, and fries. A classic fast-food choice in the park.
* **Budget:**HK$140 – $200 /person
* **Avg. Wait Time:**10-20 minutes (during peak hours).

![Royal Banquet Hall](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-royal-banquet-hall.jpg)

#### Royal Banquet Hall

* **Cuisine:**A variety of Chinese, Japanese, and Western dishes in a setting modeled after a Disney princess castle’s banquet hall.
* **Budget:**HK$140 – $200 /person
* **Avg. Wait Time:**15-30 minutes (during peak hours).

![Comet Cafe](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-comet-cafe.jpg)

#### Comet Cafe

* **Cuisine:**Japanese and Hong Kong-style set meals, such as curry pork cutlet rice and ramen.
* **Budget:**HK$140 – $200 /person
* **Avg. Wait Time:**10-20 minutes (during peak hours).

![Golden Crocus Inn](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-golden-crocus-inn.jpg)

#### Golden Crocus Inn

* **Cuisine:**A themed restaurant in the World of Frozen, offering Nordic-inspired dishes.
* **Budget:**HK$180 – $250 /person
* **Avg. Wait Time:**15-30 minutes (during peak hours).

![Wayfinders' Table](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/hkdl-dining-guide-wayfinders-table.jpg)

#### Wayfinders’ Table

* **Cuisine:**Southeast Asian dishes like Laksa, roasted chicken, and beef rendang.
* **Budget:**HK$140 – $200 /person
* **Avg. Wait Time:**10-20 minutes (during peak hours).
* **Note:** Halal-certified food is available here.

![River View Cafe](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-river-view-cafe.jpg)

#### River View Cafe

* **Cuisine:**Chinese and Asian dishes in an adventure-themed setting with outdoor seating.
* **Budget:**HK$140 – $200 /person
* **Avg. Wait Time:**10-20 minutes (during peak hours).
* **Note:** Halal-certified food is available here.

![Explorer’s Club Restaurant](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-explorers-club-restaurant.jpg)

#### Explorer’s Club Restaurant

* **Cuisine:**Themed after an explorers’ club, serving set meals from Southeast Asian, Japanese, Chinese, and Korean cuisines.
* **Budget:**HK$250 – $400 /person
* **Avg. Wait Time:**10-20 minutes (during peak hours).

If this is your first visit, you can refer to the detailed descriptions of each restaurant below to help you choose.

### Plaza Inn

Plaza Inn is the park’s dedicated Chinese restaurant. Here, you can enjoy dim sum shaped like Disney characters, Chinese desserts, and traditional Cantonese dishes. It’s a great way to experience a “yum cha” meal without leaving the park.

Expect to spend around HK$250 – HK$400 per person. Due to its limited seating, it is highly recommended to make a reservation on the Hong Kong Disneyland website in advance.

![Plaza Inn at Hong Kong Disneyland](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-plaza-inn-1024x576.jpg)

Plaza Inn at Hong Kong Disneyland

### Main Street Corner Cafe

Located on Main Street, U.S.A., this is a high-end Western restaurant. You can enjoy grilled Angus ribeye steak, wagyu cheeseburgers, and an exquisite afternoon tea set. If you time your meal right, you might even be able to watch the parade while you dine.

Set meals cost around HK$250 – $400. You can also share an afternoon tea set with a friend to experience the ambiance without committing to a full meal.

![Main Street Corner Cafe at Hong Kong Disneyland](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-main-street-corner-cafe-1024x576.jpg)

Main Street Corner Cafe at Hong Kong Disneyland

### Explorer’s Club Restaurant

Located next to Mystic Manor, this restaurant offers a semi-buffet experience. You can enjoy main courses like Indian curry, Chinese noodle soup, and pasta, with unlimited access to a selection of appetizers and desserts.

You can also book their semi-buffet dinner package that includes a ticket for the special viewing area for the “Momentous” nighttime spectacular, giving you a prime spot near the castle.

The cost is approximately HK$300 – HK$550 per person, depending on whether you dine for lunch or dinner and if the package includes the “Momentous” ticket.

![Explorer’s Club Restaurant at Hong Kong Disneyland](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-explorers-club-restaurant-1024x576.jpg)

Explorer’s Club Restaurant at Hong Kong Disneyland

### Clopin’s Festival of Foods

This is a quick-service restaurant in Fantasyland themed after the movie “The Hunchback of Notre Dame.” It mainly serves Hong Kong-style set meals like fried rice and noodle soup, which include a drink. Each set costs about HK$140 – $200, and you usually won’t have to wait long for a table.

![Clopin’s Festival of Foods at Hong Kong Disneyland](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-clopins-festival-of-foods-1024x576.jpg)

Clopin’s Festival of Foods at Hong Kong Disneyland

### Starliner Diner

This is a Marvel-themed restaurant where you can find food shaped like Iron Man, Spider-Man, and other superheroes. The menu features items like wagyu cheeseburgers, crispy fried chicken, and sides like fries and onion rings. A set meal costs around HK$140 – $200. This place is very popular, so arrive early to find a seat during peak hours.

![Starliner Diner at Hong Kong Disneyland](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-starliner-diner-1024x576.jpg)

Starliner Diner at Hong Kong Disneyland

### Royal Banquet Hall

Located near the castle, this restaurant is themed after Disney princesses. The menu includes Western sets (like baked pork chop rice, pizza, roasted chicken) and Japanese sets (like grilled fish and beef udon). Each set includes a drink and costs about HK$140 – $200. To avoid long queues, try to have lunch before 11:30 AM or dinner before 5:30 PM.

![Royal Banquet Hall at Hong Kong Disneyland](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-royal-banquet-hall-1024x576.jpg)

Royal Banquet Hall at Hong Kong Disneyland

 ![Interior of the Royal Banquet Hall at Hong Kong Disneyland](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-royal-banquet-hall-interior-1024x576.jpg)

Interior of the Royal Banquet Hall at Hong Kong Disneyland

### Comet Cafe

Located in Tomorrowland, this restaurant mainly serves Japanese-style ramen and udon noodle sets, with each costing around HK$140 – $200. Since most of its seating is outdoors, it tends to be less crowded during summer and winter, making it easier to find a table.

![Comet Cafe at Hong Kong Disneyland](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-comet-cafe-1024x576.jpg)

Comet Cafe at Hong Kong Disneyland

### Golden Crocus Inn

Located in the World of Frozen, this restaurant offers Nordic-inspired dishes like lamb shank and salmon, served with mashed potatoes and carrots, making you feel like you’re dining in the kingdom of Arendelle. The decor is also themed after the movie, featuring photos of Elsa and Anna.

![Golden Crocus Inn at Hong Kong Disneyland](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-golden-crocus-inn-1024x576.jpg)

Golden Crocus Inn at Hong Kong Disneyland

This restaurant is one of the pricier options in the park, with the cheapest set costing around $180 and the most expensive around $250. It’s best to arrive early for lunch (before 11:30 AM) or dinner (before 5:00 PM) to avoid long queues. Consider buying a meal voucher in advance for a discount.

![Interior of the Golden Crocus Inn at Hong Kong Disneyland](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-golden-crocus-inn-interior-1024x576.jpg)

Interior of the Golden Crocus Inn at Hong Kong Disneyland

Book Hong Kong Disneyland meal vouchers and tickets for extra discounts!

[Book Discounted Meal Vouchers](https://www.klook.com/en-US/activity/524-disneyland-gourmet-meal-voucher-hong-kong/?aid=97028) [Book Discounted Park Tickets](https://www.klook.com/en-US/activity/39-hong-kong-disneyland-resort-hong-kong/?aid=97028)

### Wayfinders’ Table

Located near the Theater in the Wild, this restaurant serves various Asian dishes like laksa, roasted chicken, and beef rendang. Its key feature is that all its meat dishes are Halal-certified by The Incorporated Trustees of the Islamic Community Fund of Hong Kong. Since most of its seating is outdoors, it tends to be less crowded during summer and winter.

![Wayfinders' Table](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/hkdl-dining-guide-wayfinders-table-1024x576.jpg)

Wayfinders’ Table at Hong Kong Disneyland

### River View Cafe

Situated next to the Jungle River Cruise, this restaurant allows you to watch others embark on their jungle adventure while you dine. It serves Southeast Asian cuisine such as laksa, Indian curry, and Nasi Lemak, with set meals costing around HK$140 – $200.

![River View Cafe at Hong Kong Disneyland](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-river-view-cafe-1024x576.jpg)

River View Cafe at Hong Kong Disneyland

## Snack Kiosks: Grab-and-Go Recommendations

Besides the main restaurants, you can also grab a bite from the snack kiosks located near the rides and theaters. This allows you to eat while queuing, saving more time for attractions.

There are about 15 snack kiosks in the park selling simple items like popcorn and ice cream bars. Some also offer specialty items like grilled squid, turkey legs, and themed desserts. If you want a deeper “Disney experience,” these kiosks are not to be missed.

#### Specialty Snack Kiosks at Hong Kong Disneyland

![Jessie’s Snack Roundup](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-jessies-snack-roundup.jpg)

###### Jessie’s Snack Roundup

A themed food truck in Toy Story Land serving American-style hot dogs and drinks.

![Main Street Market](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-main-street-market.jpg)

###### Main Street Market

Located on Main Street, U.S.A., this is the most popular kiosk, offering Mickey-shaped waffles and Hong Kong-style egg puffs.

![Karibuni Marketplace](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-karibuni-marketplace.jpg)

###### Karibuni Marketplace

Located in Adventureland, offering grilled corn on the cob, hot dogs, and specialty craft beers.

![Market House Bakery](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-market-house-cafe.jpg)

###### Market House Bakery

This is a Starbucks-operated cafe where you can get various coffees, pastries, and exclusive Starbucks x Disney merchandise.

![NRG POD](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-nrg-pod.jpg)

###### NRG POD

Located in Tomorrowland, selling roasted turkey legs and various drinks and snacks.

![Main Street Bakery](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-main-street-bakery.jpg)

###### Main Street Bakery

Operated by Maxim’s Group, this bakery offers a variety of cakes and desserts shaped like Disney characters.

![Small World Ice Cream](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-small-world-ice-cream.jpg)

###### Small World Ice Cream

Located in Fantasyland, selling limited-edition ice cream and frozen treats.

![Midtown Delights](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-midtown-delights.jpg)

###### Midtown Delights

Located on Main Street, U.S.A., specializing in seasonal ice cream flavors.

![Northern Delights](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-northern-delights.jpg)

###### Northern Delights

A candy shop in the World of Frozen, selling exquisite movie-themed candies and desserts, such as Olaf-shaped ice cream.

| Food Type | Locations & Features |
| --- | --- |
| Popcorn | * Grizzly Gulch Popcorn Cart * Fantasyland Popcorn Cart * Tomorrowland Popcorn Cart * Main Street, U.S.A. Popcorn Cart |
| Ice Cream Bars | * Fantasyland Frozen Lollipops Cart * Adventureland Frozen Lollipops Cart * Tomorrowland Frozen Lollipops Cart * Mystic Point Frozen Lollipops Cart |
| Other Specialty Snacks | * **Lucky Nugget Saloon:** Located in Grizzly Gulch, offers desserts and pastries. * **Adventureland:** You can find grilled squid and giant turkey legs here. * **Jungle Junction:** Located in Adventureland, offers snacks. * **Main Street, U.S.A. dessert shops:** Offer various ice creams and desserts. |

For first-time visitors, I highly recommend trying the following snacks:

* Grilled Squid from Karibuni Marketplace
* Olaf-shaped Ice Cream from Northern Delights
* Ice Cream from Small World Ice Cream
* Ice Cream from Midtown Delights
* Ice Cream from Lucky Nugget Saloon

These snacks are specially designed and perfect for photos to commemorate your visit.

![Various desserts at Hong Kong Disneyland](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-special-sweets-1024x576.jpg)

Various desserts at Hong Kong Disneyland

If you’re looking to save money, consider visiting Market House Bakery or Main Street Bakery. You can get a coffee and a pastry for around HK$60 – HK$80. Their location on Main Street, U.S.A. makes them a convenient place to grab something while waiting for the parade.

![The bakery inside Hong Kong Disneyland](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-bakery-coffee-explain-1024x576.jpg)

The bakery inside Hong Kong Disneyland

## The “Yum Cha” Experience at Hong Kong Disneyland

Since you’re in Hong Kong, you shouldn’t miss the chance to experience “yum cha.” There are two places in the resort where you can enjoy traditional Cantonese dim sum:

1. Inside the Park: Plaza Inn
2. At the Hong Kong Disneyland Hotel: Crystal Lotus

### Plaza Inn

Plaza Inn offers classic Cantonese dim sum and dishes. For a truly magical experience, book a table at Jade Garden (within Plaza Inn), which serves exquisite dim sum shaped like Disney characters. Expect to spend HK$250 – HK$400 per person.

![Plaza Inn at Hong Kong Disneyland](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-plaza-inn-1024x576.jpg)

Plaza Inn at Hong Kong Disneyland

### Crystal Lotus

Located in the Hong Kong Disneyland Hotel, Crystal Lotus serves exquisite Cantonese dim sum. They offer a refined lunch set that includes dim sum, seasonal soup, stir-fried dishes, fried rice, and dessert. You’ll need to make reservations in advance and budget around HK$400 – HK$600 per person.

![Crystal Lotus at the Hong Kong Disneyland Hotel](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-crystal-lotus-1024x576.jpg)

Crystal Lotus at the Hong Kong Disneyland Hotel

## Restaurants at the Hong Kong Disneyland Hotels

Besides the in-park restaurants, the resort hotels also have several dining options, including bars, buffets, and cafes, which are typically open until 10:30 PM. They are a great choice for dinner after a full day in the park.

Reservations are highly recommended, and you should budget around HK$400 – HK$600 per person for a fulfilling dinner.

![Enchanted Garden Restaurant](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-enchanted-garden-restaurant.jpg)

#### Enchanted Garden Restaurant

* **Hotel:**Hong Kong Disneyland Hotel
* **Cuisine:**International Buffet
* **Dining Style:**Buffet
* **Feature:**The most popular character dining experience where you can meet and take photos with Disney characters. Reservations are essential.

![Walt's Cafe](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-walts-cafe.jpg)

#### Walt’s Cafe

* **Hotel:**Hong Kong Disneyland Hotel
* **Cuisine:**International, Western
* **Dining Style:**A La Carte / Set Menu
* **Feature:**Inspired by the life of Walt Disney, this elegant cafe offers exquisite Western dishes and afternoon tea.

![Crystal Lotus](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-crystal-lotus.jpg)

#### Crystal Lotus

* **Hotel:**Hong Kong Disneyland Hotel
* **Cuisine:**Cantonese, Chinese
* **Dining Style:**A La Carte / Set Menu
* **Feature:**Famous for its exquisite Disney character-themed dim sum, which must be ordered in advance.

![Ink & Plate](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-chef-mickey.jpg)

#### Ink & Plate

* **Hotel:**Disney’s Hollywood Hotel
* **Cuisine:**International Buffet
* **Dining Style:**Buffet
* **Feature:**A Hollywood-themed restaurant where you can meet and take photos with Chef Mickey. Reservations are essential.

![The Archivist](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-hollywood-and-dine.jpg)

#### The Archivist

* **Hotel:**Disney’s Hollywood Hotel
* **Cuisine:**International Quick Service
* **Dining Style:**Quick Service
* **Feature:**A casual dining environment offering a variety of choices, including burgers, pasta, and Chinese rice and noodle dishes.

![Dragon Wind](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-dragon-wind.jpg)

#### Dragon Wind

* **Hotel:**Disney Explorers Lodge
* **Cuisine:**Chinese & International Buffet
* **Dining Style:**Buffet
* **Feature:**Decorated with dragon and kite themes, this restaurant offers a character dining experience. Reservations are essential.

![World of Color Restaurant](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-world-of-color-restaurant.jpg)

#### World of Color Restaurant

* **Hotel:**Disney Explorers Lodge
* **Cuisine:**International, Western
* **Dining Style:**A La Carte / Set Menu
* **Feature:**A vibrant restaurant offering a variety of exquisite Western dishes, perfect for a leisurely meal.

![Chart Room Cafe](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-chart-room-cafe.jpg)

#### Chart Room Cafe

* **Hotel:**Disney Explorers Lodge
* **Cuisine:**Light meals, desserts, coffee
* **Dining Style:**Cafe
* **Feature:**A relaxing cafe offering sandwiches, salads, cakes, and more.

Book Hong Kong Disneyland tickets and dining packages for extra discounts!

[Book Discounted Tickets](https://www.klook.com/en-US/activity/39-hong-kong-disneyland-resort-hong-kong/?aid=97028)  
 [HK Disneyland Official Website](https://www.hongkongdisneyland.com/)

## Dining Discounts at Hong Kong Disneyland

Dining in the park isn’t cheap. So, are there any ways to save on your food budget?

Based on my experience, you can get dining discounts through several methods:

1. Purchase discounted meal vouchers
2. Buy promotional packages for specific restaurants
3. Use a Hong Kong Disneyland Annual Pass (Magic Access)

These methods can save you anywhere from 5% to 25% on your food expenses.

### 1. Purchase Discounted Meal Vouchers

On travel platforms like Klook and KKday, you can easily find “Hong Kong Disneyland Resort Meal Vouchers” that offer nearly a 20% discount on a package that includes lunch, dinner, and a designated snack.

![Discounted meal vouchers for Hong Kong Disneyland](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-park-meal-coupon-explain-1024x576.jpg)

Discounted meal vouchers for Hong Kong Disneyland

For example, a voucher from Klook might cost HK$305 but give you HK$360 in value, including two HK$160 meal coupons and one HK$40 snack coupon. You can use these coupons at designated restaurants, including popular spots like Golden Crocus Inn, Royal Banquet Hall, and Starliner Diner, and redeem the snack coupon at a kiosk for an ice cream bar or popcorn.

Book Hong Kong Disneyland Discounted Meal Vouchers

[Klook Meal Vouchers](https://www.klook.com/en-US/activity/524-disneyland-gourmet-meal-voucher-hong-kong/?aid=97028)  
 [KKday Meal Vouchers](https://www.kkday.com/en/product/20967-hong-kong-disneyland-meal-vouchers/?cid=22671)

### 2. Buy Promotional Packages for Specific Restaurants

If you’re interested in dining at the resort hotels—especially for character dining—you can often find promotional packages. Three restaurants—Crystal Lotus (for dim sum), Enchanted Garden Restaurant (for dinner buffet), and Ink & Plate (for dinner buffet)—frequently offer packages that can save you 3% to 10% when you book in advance.

![Promotional packages for designated restaurants at Hong Kong Disneyland hotels](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-dining-guide-meal-discount-explain-1024x576.jpg)

Promotional packages for designated restaurants at Hong Kong Disneyland hotels

Book Promotional Packages for Designated Disneyland Restaurants

[Crystal Lotus](https://www.kkday.com/en/product/100495-disneyland-dim-sum-lunch-crystal-lotus-hong-kong/?cid=22671)  
 [Enchanted Garden Restaurant](https://www.klook.com/en-US/activity/137612-hong-kong-disneyland-enchanted-garden-restaurant/?aid=97028)  
 [Ink & Plate](https://www.klook.com/en-US/activity/137617-hong-kong-disneyland-ink-plate/?aid=97028)

### 3. Use a Hong Kong Disneyland Annual Pass (Magic Access)

Another way to save on dining is by using a Magic Access pass, which offers discounts of up to 25% at designated restaurants in the park and hotels.

![Hong Kong Disneyland Annual Pass: Magic Access](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/hkdl-magic-access-guide-pass-demo-1024x576.jpg)

Hong Kong Disneyland Annual Pass: Magic Access

If your trip is centered around visiting the park and staying at the resort hotels, consider buying an annual pass or upgrading your 1-Day ticket to get the maximum discounts.

**More Info:** [Magic Access Detailed Benefits and Guide](https://en.kowloonvibes.com/hong-kong/article/travel/disney/hkdl-magic-access-guide/)

Book your Hong Kong Disneyland tickets and enjoy extra discounts!

[Book Discounted Tickets](https://www.klook.com/en-US/activity/39-hong-kong-disneyland-resort-hong-kong/?aid=97028)  
 [HK Disneyland Official Website](https://www.hongkongdisneyland.com/)

## Frequently Asked Questions about Dining

Here are some more answers to common questions about dining at Hong Kong Disneyland.

### How much should I budget for food?

I recommend budgeting at least HK$400 per person for food. This would cover about HK$150 for lunch, HK$150 for dinner, and the remaining HK$100 for popcorn, ice cream, and other themed snacks.

### Which restaurants are best for families?

During Hong Kong’s hot and humid summers, I recommend choosing restaurants with ample indoor seating. For families, Starliner Diner, Royal Banquet Hall, and Golden Crocus Inn are all excellent quick-service options with plenty of indoor space. If you have a larger budget, consider making a reservation at Plaza Inn for a comfortable dim sum experience.

### When are the peak dining hours?

Generally, peak lunch hours are from 12:00 PM to 2:00 PM, and peak dinner hours are from 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM. During major holidays like Christmas and Chinese New Year, all restaurants will be very busy at all times, so making reservations where possible is highly recommended.

### Which restaurants are good for takeaway?

If you prefer to eat on the go, you can grab heartier items like hot dogs, grilled squid, and turkey legs from kiosks like Jessie’s Snack Roundup and Karibuni Marketplace, or get pastries from Main Street Bakery.

### Can I bring my own food into the park?

According to current park rules, guests are allowed to bring in small amounts of simple, convenient food items that do not have a strong odor and do not require heating or refrigeration. In short, you can bring some biscuits or bread, but not instant noodles, cheese, sushi, or canned food.

### Is free drinking water available?

Yes, there are free water refilling stations located throughout the park. However, during the summer, the queues can be long (15-20 minutes). It’s a good idea to bring extra water with you on hot days.

### Can I drink alcohol in the park?

Some kiosks sell craft beer, but it is expensive. The park prohibits bringing in your own alcoholic beverages, and public intoxication is handled seriously. It’s best to enjoy the park’s atmosphere and skip the alcohol for the day.

### Which restaurants offer character dining?

If you want to dine with Disney characters, your best options are at the resort hotels. Enchanted Garden Restaurant and World of Color Restaurant both offer character dining buffet experiences.

### Are there Halal-certified restaurants?

Yes. Inside the park, River View Cafe and Wayfinders’ Table offer Halal-certified meat dishes. At the hotels, Crystal Lotus, Walt’s Cafe, The Archivist, Ink & Plate, and World of Color Restaurant also offer Halal-certified food options.

## Summary

After this in-depth guide, you should have a better understanding of the dining options at Hong Kong Disneyland, including what to budget, which restaurants are available, and what kind of cuisine they serve. On your next visit, you can use this information to plan your meals and save more on your dining budget.

Furthermore, if you are searching for more information about traveling in Hong Kong, feel free to explore this website in depth. I have prepared a variety of articles on Hong Kong travel topics to provide you with the most in-depth and practical travel information.

Explore More Hong Kong Travel Guides!

results.title: How Much a Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs + Budget - The Disney Specialist
results.content:
The Early Park Entry Pass is a pass you can buy on top of your tickets or Magic Access memberships that grants you an extra hour in the park in the morning. How it works is everyone who bought that pass for the day will be able to get into the park 1 hour before everyone else. Some shops, food stands, and attractions will be open at this time for you to enjoy, including Frozen Ever After, Wandering Oaken’s Sliding Sleighs, Dumbo, the Carousel, and The Many Adventures of Winnie the Pooh (as of January 2026). The pass costs HK$199 or US$25.52 per person. [...] Hong Kong Disneyland Costs iron man burger

Dinner buffets go from HK$568 or US$72.86 to HK$818 or US$104.93 per adult and HK$368 or US$47.20 to HK$508 or US$65.16 per child. 

Lunch buffets cost HK$418 or US$53.62 to HK$498 or US$63.88 per adult and HK$268 or US$34.38 to HK$328 or US$42.07 per child.

Main courses at The Archivist, Walt’s Cafe, Crystal Lotus, World of Color, and Dragon Wind go from HK$188 or US$24.12 to as high as HK$1,600 or US$205.24. However, the main courses, on average, are about HK$220 or US$28.22 to HK$280 or US$35.92. 

Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs banquet

Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs banquet

Alcoholic cocktails usually go for HK$120 or US$15.39 to HK$150 or US$19.24, with bottles and glasses of drinks like champagne costing more. [...] The Crystal Lotus Disney Dim Sum Lunch Set goes for HK$428 or US$54.90 per person. This is the dining experience where you can eat dim sum that looks like various Disney characters. If you’d like to participate in a dim sum making class in addition to the dim sum lunch, that will cost HK$1,388 or US$178.04 per person.
results.raw_content:
![The Disney Specialist](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/DS-full-logo_954x736-WB.png.webp)
![The Disney Specialist Logo](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/ds-mobile-logo.png.webp)

## How Much a Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs + Budget

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs cover](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/HKDL-Trip-Report-2023-Part-One-Introduction-79.png "HKDL Trip Report 2023 Part One Introduction (79)")

I’ll be honest, I’ve been putting off writing this post. Not because I thought it wouldn’t be helpful to people, but more because I couldn’t figure out how to write it. Costing a Hong Kong Disneyland (HKDL) trip is so specific to each individual person. Every person has different budgets and interests, so how can I possibly make a costing post that would tackle all of them at once. Well, I think I’ve finally found a format that works, and so this Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs and Budget post was born. 

Believe it or not, despite Hong Kong being a somewhat expensive city, Hong Kong Disneyland is actually one of the cheapest Disney resorts in the world. It has one of the lowest park ticket and annual pass prices, as well as relatively affordable nightly hotel rates, although the food is more expensive than those of the neighboring Disney Parks in Tokyo and Shanghai. 

![Hong Kong Disneyland costs me](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/photo_2025-12-17-17.16.35-400x533.jpeg)

![Hong Kong Disneyland costs me](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/photo_2025-12-17-17.16.35-400x533.jpeg)

But even with prices being relatively low, I know some people would still like to know in advance how much they’re going to have to pay for their Disney trip. Hopefully this post can be helpful for those who want to price and budget their trip to Hong Kong Disneyland ahead of time so they know what to expect. 

This post will go into how much each aspect of your Hong Kong Disneyland vacation will cost, from hotels to tickets to transport to merchandise to food. I decided that I will first lay out how much each particular category costs, and then will go into a sample budget, picking and choosing what to include in the sample budget based on what I think the typical Hong Kong Disneyland trip looks like. 

Let’s dive in!

*This post contains affiliate links. We receive a small commission for purchases made after clicking on these links at no extra cost to you.*

**Click the links to buy discounted tickets to:**

[**Disneyland**](https://klook.tpm.li/1NxizHeB)

**[Walt Disney World](https://klook.tpm.li/YzxReBQe)**

**[Tokyo Disney](https://klook.tpm.li/OhqyVvNC)**

**[Disneyland Paris](https://klook.tpm.li/xIl2V7Zd)**

[**Hong Kong Disneyland**](https://klook.tpm.li/JtibgsSR)

**[Shanghai Disneyland](https://klook.tpm.li/35t86XI7)**

**Click the links to buy discounted hotel stays and dining experiences:**

**[Hong Kong Disneyland Dining Offers](https://klook.tpm.li/9lcIRTFk)**

[**Hong Kong Disneyland Hotel**](https://klook.tpm.li/CrPw7hf6)

**[Disney Explorers Lodge](https://klook.tpm.li/XKSd3yKW)**

**[Disney’s Hollywood Hotel](https://klook.tpm.li/mHxofW0S)**

Click the following links for planning posts related to Hong Kong Disneyland!

**Like me on [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/TheDisneySpecialist)** **and follow me on** [**Instagram**](https://www.instagram.com/stephenthedisneygeek/) **/** [**Twitter**](https://twitter.com/StephenJFlores) **/** [**Tiktok!**](https://www.tiktok.com/@stephenthedisneygeek)

Table of Contents

## **Airfare**

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs ever after](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/photo_2025-11-10-22.07.49-600x450.jpeg)

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs ever after](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/photo_2025-11-10-22.07.49-600x450.jpeg)

This is the first thing we’re going into, mostly because it’s the first thing people normally book before going on vacation. The cost of flights will depend on three factors: time of year you’re traveling, the kind of airline you’re flying, and the class you decide to purchase a ticket for. For the sake of this post, I’m going to give you a range of the cost of flying to Hong Kong from different parts of the world. 

If you’re flying to Hong Kong on a short-haul flight from nearby places like the Philippines, Thailand, Singapore, Taiwan, Japan, South Korea, or Mainland China, expect to pay around US$100 – US$350 for a roundtrip ticket. If you fly on budget airlines like HKExpress, AirAsia, or Cebu Pacific, expect to pay something on the lower end of this range. Tickets for non-budget airlines like Cathay Pacific, EVA Air, Japan Airlines, and Korean Air will be on the higher end of this range.

![Hong Kong Disneyland Costs disney friends live](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/photo_2025-11-10-22.04.51-400x533.jpeg)

![Hong Kong Disneyland Costs disney friends live](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/photo_2025-11-10-22.04.51-400x533.jpeg)

This range is only for Economy tickets. If you would like to fly business class to Hong Kong (which I don’t recommend if you’re coming from someplace reachable via a short-haul flight), I would expect the cost of your ticket to double. But again, I wouldn’t recommend doing this for short-haul flights. 

Now if you need a long-haul flight to get to Hong Kong (like if you’re coming from Europe, the Middle East, Australia, New Zealand, and North America), then expect to pay anywhere from US$500 – US$1,600 for Economy seats, depending on where you’re coming. The lower end is for flights coming from Australia, with the more expensive side of the range is for flights coming from Europe and North America.

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs albert](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/photo_2025-07-16-15.29.16-600x450.jpeg)

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs albert](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/photo_2025-07-16-15.29.16-600x450.jpeg)

Buying Business Class tickets for long haul flights can set you back thousands of dollars depending on the airline and the dates of your trip. It might be worth it for long haul flights, but it will make up more than 70% of your trip costs if you decide to fly business class. I never fly business class as I’d like to save my money for other things during the trip. 

When buying flights, I have two tips for you: buy well in advance, and buy connecting flights. Buying in advance ensures that you get the cheapest price possible. I would also wait for sales from airlines to happen as they can greatly reduce the price of your trip. Another hack I’ve figured out over the years from buying flights is that buying connecting flights is usually cheaper than buying direct flights. This doesn’t make a lot of sense to me but it’s true. If you can swing it, transiting through another city or country could make more financial sense for you.   

[Klook.com](//www.klook.com/)   

## **Transportation**

![Hong Kong Disneyland Costs rc](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/photo_2025-07-16-14.19.09-600x450.jpeg.webp)

![Hong Kong Disneyland Costs rc](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/photo_2025-07-16-14.19.09-600x450.jpeg.webp)

I have an [HKDL Transportation Guide](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/how-to-get-to-hong-kong-disneyland-from-the-airport-and-the-city/) that goes deeper into transportation options going to and from the resort in case you would like to read something more in depth about your transport options. 

You’ll usually come from one of two places when going to Hong Kong Disneyland: from the Hong Kong International Airport or from Hong Kong city or Downtown Hong Kong. In my mind, there are two convenient ways to get to and from both of these locations: by Taxi or by MTR. I’ll break down the costs of each below.

If you’re coming or going to Hong Kong Disneyland from the Airport, it’ll cost you around HK$150 (US$19.24) to HK$250 (US$32.07) to get there in a taxi, depending on how much luggage you have. If you can fit into one taxi, then taking a taxi is more economical as you can split the cost of the taxi. This is my preferred method of transport if you’re coming from the airport and going to the resort and vice versa. You’re going to want to take the blue taxi if you’re coming from the airport, but you can take any colored taxi from the resort to the airport.  

![](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/photo_2025-07-16-14.14.46-600x450.jpeg.webp)

![](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/photo_2025-07-16-14.14.46-600x450.jpeg.webp)

On the other hand, taking the MTR or metro from the airport to the resort can be a bit tricky and will require two transfers. First, you’ll need to take the Airport Express MTR to Tsing Yi, transfer to the Tung Chung line and go to Sunny Bay Station, and then transfer from there to the Disneyland Resort Line. From there you can take a resort shuttle to your hotel. It’ll cost anywhere from HK$88 (US$11.29) to HK$97 (US$12.44) to go one way from the airport to the resort or from the resort to the airport. If you’re traveling solo or with one other person, it would make more sense to take the metro cost-wise. Time-wise, however, taking the taxi is much faster and you would have to carry your luggage during your transfers on the MTR. 

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs dumbo](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/photo_2025-07-16-14.16.20-600x450.jpeg.webp)

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs dumbo](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/photo_2025-07-16-14.16.20-600x450.jpeg.webp)

Now to get from the city to Hong Kong Disneyland, you have those same two options of taking a taxi or taking the MTR. A taxi from downtown Hong Kong will cost you anywhere from HK$200 (US$25.65) – HK$350 (US$44.90) depending again on how many bags you have and how many taxis you’ll need if you have a larger party. This is better for those that want to save time as it takes only about 30-50 minutes to get to HKDL from the city, depending on the traffic. 

Taking the MTR from the city to the park and back will set you back anywhere from HK$15 (US$1.92) to HK$35 (US$4.49) depending on where you’re coming from. It’s a lot cheaper than using the MTR to get to the resort from the airport because you don’t use the costly Airport Express train line, which adds about HK$60 to your journey. The downside to this is that it’ll take longer to get to the resort, sometimes taking up to an hour or more. 

## **Park Tickets**

![Hong Kong Disneyland Costs playhouse](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/photo_2025-07-15-23.02.13-600x450.jpeg.webp)

![Hong Kong Disneyland Costs playhouse](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/photo_2025-07-15-23.02.13-600x450.jpeg.webp)

I have a [Hong Kong Disneyland Ticket Guide](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/hong-kong-disneyland-tickets-guide/) that goes in depth on regular park tickets and Magic Access passes. 

After buying your airfare, the next thing you need to concern yourself with is buying park tickets. If you’re planning on going to Hong Kong Disneyland for 1 or 2 days, I would recommend buying regular park tickets. 

1-day tickets start at HK$669 (US$86.23) per adult and HK$499 (US$64.32) per child/senior. This goes up to HK$849 (US$109.43) per adult and HK$639 (US$82.36) per child/senior depending on when you visit as each day is assigned a specific tier depending on how crowded HKDL thinks that day is going to be. The more crowded, the more expensive the ticket. A 2-day ticket costs HK$1,132 (US$145.90) per adult and HK$849 (US$109.43) per child/senior. 2-day ticket prices stay the same year round.

![](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/photo_2025-07-14-17.51.00-600x450.jpeg.webp)

![](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/photo_2025-07-14-17.51.00-600x450.jpeg.webp)

Before you buy tickets, always do your due diligence and check other websites such as Klook and the special offers page on the Hong Kong Disneyland website as there are usually ticket deals happening at any given time. Third party websites such as Klook also usually sell tickets for cheaper than the HKDL website price. If you want to lower your Hong Kong Disneyland trip costs and budget, always be on the lookout for deals!

**Click the links to buy discounted tickets to:**

[**Disneyland**](https://klook.tpm.li/1NxizHeB)

**[Walt Disney World](https://klook.tpm.li/YzxReBQe)**

**[Tokyo Disney](https://klook.tpm.li/OhqyVvNC)**

**[Disneyland Paris](https://klook.tpm.li/xIl2V7Zd)**

[**Hong Kong Disneyland**](https://klook.tpm.li/JtibgsSR)

**[Shanghai Disneyland](https://klook.tpm.li/35t86XI7)**

**Click the links to buy discounted hotel stays and dining experiences:**

**[Hong Kong Disneyland Dining Offers](https://klook.tpm.li/9lcIRTFk)**

[**Hong Kong Disneyland Hotel**](https://klook.tpm.li/CrPw7hf6)

**[Disney Explorers Lodge](https://klook.tpm.li/XKSd3yKW)**

**[Disney’s Hollywood Hotel](https://klook.tpm.li/mHxofW0S)**

## **Magic Access**

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs friendtastic](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/photo_2025-07-11-14.18.46-400x533.jpeg.webp)

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs friendtastic](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/photo_2025-07-11-14.18.46-400x533.jpeg.webp)

If you’re visiting for 3 or more days, or plan to visit the resort again in less than a year, it may make more sense to buy a Magic Access pass. Magic Access is Hong Kong Disneyland’s annual passholder program. It comes in three tiers: Silver, Gold, and Platinum. Each Magic Access tier comes with unique benefits including, but not limited to, discounts on shopping and dining, discounts on hotel stays, and access to exclusive Magic Access events. 

If you’d like to learn more about the benefits of each Magic Access tier, read my [Hong Kong Disneyland ticket guide](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/hong-kong-disneyland-tickets-guide/).

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs BGMRMC](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/photo_2025-07-16-14.27.38-400x533.jpeg)

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs BGMRMC](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/photo_2025-07-16-14.27.38-400x533.jpeg)

The silver magic access pass is currently priced at (as of January 2026) HK$1,568 or US$201.67 for people aged 12-59 and HK$1,178 or US$151.51 for Children aged 3-11, Students aged 12-25, and Seniors 60 and over.

The Gold Magic Access pass currently costs HK$2,748 or US$353.43 for people aged 12-59 and HK$2,058 or US$264.69 for Children aged 3-11, Students aged 12-25, and Seniors 60 and over.

The Platinum magic access pass costs HK$4,998 or US$642.82 for people aged 12-59 and HK$3,748 or US$482.05 for Children aged 3-11, Students aged 12-25, and Seniors 60 and over.

## **Hotels**

![](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/IMAGE-2025-07-17-143110-400x533.jpg)

![](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/IMAGE-2025-07-17-143110-400x533.jpg)

Hong Kong Disneyland has 3 resort hotels: the Hong Kong Disneyland Hotel, the Disney Explorers Lodge, and Disney’s Hollywood Hotel. They go from most expensive to least expensive in that order as well. I’ve had the opportunity to stay at each hotel, and they’re all amazing accommodation options for your trip. 

Hong Kong Disneyland Hotel is the flagship hotel at Hong Kong Disneyland. With 400 rooms, it’s the smallest of the three hotels. It has a classic Victorian theme and is the most expensive of the three hotels. The nightly cost for the cheapest room starts at HK$1,750 or US$224.48 if you buy a standard room with a special discount offer, and can go as high as HK$30,000 or US$2,848.18 per night for the Walt Disney suite. 

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs explorers lodge](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/photo_2025-11-12-21.33.37-600x450.jpeg)

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs explorers lodge](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/photo_2025-11-12-21.33.37-600x450.jpeg)

[Disney Explorers Lodge](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/disney-explorers-lodge-hotel-review/) is Hong Kong Disneyland’s most recently built hotel. It has 750 rooms, making it the biggest of the three hotels. It takes its design inspirations from the predominant architectural styles of Asia, Oceania, Africa, and South America. It’s in the middle of the road price-wise. Nightly room rates go from HK$1,470 or US$188.56 for a standard room with a special discount offer, and can go as high as HK$13,000 or US$1,667.54 for a suite. 

![](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/IMAGE-2025-07-21-231437-600x450.jpg)

![](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/IMAGE-2025-07-21-231437-600x450.jpg)

Lastly, [Disney’s Hollywood Hotel](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/disneys-hollywood-hotel-hong-kong-disneyland-review-is-it-worth-staying-at/) is the second largest hotel on property with 600 rooms. It has an art deco design and is themed around Hollywood and movie making. It has recently renovated rooms with Marvel and Mickey and Friends themes, and has a Marvel-themed bar adjacent to its lobby. It’s the cheapest of the three hotels. Nightly prices go from HK$1,190 or US$152.64 for standard rooms with a special discount offer, and can go as high as HK$2,450 or US$314.27 for a Sea View room without any special offers. This is the only hotel that does not have suites. 

## **Early Park Entry Passes and Premier Access Passes**

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs entrance](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/photo_2025-07-16-14.18.37-400x533.jpeg.webp)

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs entrance](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/photo_2025-07-16-14.18.37-400x533.jpeg.webp)

If you’d like more in depth guides on these special passes, click to read my guides on the [Early Park Entry Pass](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/early-park-entry-pass-at-hong-kong-disneyland-guide-is-it-worth-it/) and the [Premier Access Pass](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/premier-access-at-hong-kong-disneyland-guide-is-it-worth-it/). I go into whether these passes are worth purchasing in these posts, so I won’t go into too much detail on that. But I will describe each and tell you how much they cost below. 

The Early Park Entry Pass is a pass you can buy on top of your tickets or Magic Access memberships that grants you an extra hour in the park in the morning. How it works is everyone who bought that pass for the day will be able to get into the park 1 hour before everyone else. Some shops, food stands, and attractions will be open at this time for you to enjoy, including [Frozen Ever After](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/frozen-ever-after-hkdl-attraction-review/), Wandering Oaken’s Sliding Sleighs, Dumbo, the Carousel, and The Many Adventures of Winnie the Pooh (as of January 2026). The pass costs HK$199 or US$25.52 per person. 

![](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/photo_2025-07-16-14.15.35-600x450.jpeg.webp)

![](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/photo_2025-07-16-14.15.35-600x450.jpeg.webp)

Premier Access, on the other hand, is Hong Kong Disneyland’s premium skip the line service. When you purchase Premier Access, you get access to a shortened queue for certain attractions and shows. They come in single attraction passes, as well as in bundles that contain either 3, 8, or 11 attractions. They vary in price and can be used at any time during their validity. 

Single attraction passes are currently priced at either HK$89 or US$11.42, HK$99 or US$12.70, and HK$109 or US$13.98 depending on the attraction. The first bundle, the 3 attractions bundle, costs HK$199 or US$25.53. The 8 attractions bundle, on the other hand, costs HK$429 or US$55.03. Finally, the 11 attractions pass costs HK$659 or US$84.43. For a full list of attractions and entertainment offering Premier Access and their respective prices, read my [Premier Access guide](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/premier-access-at-hong-kong-disneyland-guide-is-it-worth-it/). 

## **Dining at Hong Kong Disneyland**

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs wayfinders](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/photo_2025-12-17-17.16.31-600x450.jpeg)

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs wayfinders](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/photo_2025-12-17-17.16.31-600x450.jpeg)

Hong Kong Disneyland has a plethora of dining options available throughout the resort. It would be a herculean task to list the price of every single food item in every single cart, restaurant, and food stand in the park. So instead, I’m going to give you an estimate of how much you’re going to pay for food. 

Snacks in the park will cost anywhere from HK$40 or US$5.13 to $HK95 or US$12.19, with the most expensive snack costing HK$160 or US$20.52  (the turkey leg). Meals at quick service restaurants will cost you anywhere from HK$140 or US$17.96 to HK$220 or US$28.22 depending on the restaurant. Kids meals at quick service restaurants typically go for HK$120 or US$15.39 to HK$140 or US$17.96. Afternoon tea at the Main Street Corner Cafe costs HK$488 or US$62.60 for two people, with the main courses at the aforementioned restaurant and Plaza Inn costing anywhere from HK$240 or US$30.79 to HK$400 or US$51.31. 

![](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/photo_2025-12-17-17.16.38-1-600x450.jpeg)

![](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/photo_2025-12-17-17.16.38-1-600x450.jpeg)

At the hotels, meals at the sole hotel quick service place (Chart Room Cafe) cost anywhere from HK$148 or US$18.98 to HK$168 or US$21.55 for breakfast and HK$158 or US$20.27 to HK$188 or US$24.12 for non-breakfast meals. The afternoon tea at World of Color in the Disney Explorers Lodge costs HK$488 or US$62.60 for two people, while it costs HK$688 or US$88.25 to do the afternoon tea in the Grand Salon in the Hong Kong Disneyland Hotel for two people.

Character breakfast buffets at the hotels go from HK$280 or US$35.92 to HK$358 or US$45.92 per adult and HK$208 or US$26.68 to HK$248 or US$31.81 per child. 

![Hong Kong Disneyland Costs iron man burger](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/photo_2025-12-17-17.16.42-600x450.jpeg)

![Hong Kong Disneyland Costs iron man burger](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/photo_2025-12-17-17.16.42-600x450.jpeg)

Dinner buffets go from HK$568 or US$72.86 to HK$818 or US$104.93 per adult and HK$368 or US$47.20 to HK$508 or US$65.16 per child. 

Lunch buffets cost HK$418 or US$53.62 to HK$498 or US$63.88 per adult and HK$268 or US$34.38 to HK$328 or US$42.07 per child.

Main courses at The Archivist, Walt’s Cafe, Crystal Lotus, World of Color, and Dragon Wind go from HK$188 or US$24.12 to as high as HK$1,600 or US$205.24. However, the main courses, on average, are about HK$220 or US$28.22 to HK$280 or US$35.92. 

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs banquet](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/photo_2025-12-17-17.16.46-600x450.jpeg)

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs banquet](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/photo_2025-12-17-17.16.46-600x450.jpeg)

Alcoholic cocktails usually go for HK$120 or US$15.39 to HK$150 or US$19.24, with bottles and glasses of drinks like champagne costing more. 

All in all, I would budget around HK$170 or US$21.81 to HK$250 or US$32.07 per person for a quick service meal at the parks, assuming everyone in your party is an adult. 

![](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/photo_2025-12-17-17.16.45-600x450.jpeg)

![](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/photo_2025-12-17-17.16.45-600x450.jpeg)

Table service meals will go for HK$220 or US$28.22 to HK$340 or US$43.61 per person, while hotel table service meals will cost HK$250 or US$32.07 to HK$400 or US$51.31. 

There are a couple more dining experiences I have yet to talk about as I’m saving them for the special experiences portion of this Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs post. 

## **Merchandise**

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs iasw](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/photo_2025-07-16-14.26.05-600x450.jpeg)

![Hong Kong Disneyland Trip Costs iasw](https://thedisneyspecialist.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/photo_2025-07-16-14.26.05-600x450.jpeg)

Merchandise prices at Hong Kong Disneyland will vary based on the kind of merchandise. You could pay as little as HK$15 or US$1.92 for a pressed penny or HK$500 or US$64.14 to HK$700 or US$89.79 for something like a spirit jersey or a nice figurine. Jewelry prices from Pandora can go even higher. 

I would say that if you’re not a huge Disney fan, I would budget around HK$300 or US$38.48 to HK$800 or US$102.62 for merchandise. Obviously if you’re a huge Disney fan, you’re going to want to spend more than that so in that case I would allocate more money to your merchandise budget, maybe around HK$700 or US$89.79 to HK$1,500 or US$192.41. HKDL has some really good merchandise, probably second only to Tokyo Disney in quality and variety, so I would plan on spending some amount of money for this.
